title: Reporting a Lost Item
summary: A passenger reports a lost handbag to a transport police officer, describing the bag and its contents so the officer can file a report.

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: Excuse me, officer. I think I left my handbag on the train.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: I'm sorry to hear that. When did you notice it was missing?

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: About ten minutes ago, when I arrived at the station exit.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Which train were you on — do you remember the number or the line?

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: I was on the Circle Line. I got off at Victoria Station about fifteen minutes ago.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Thank you. Can you describe the bag for me?

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: It's a medium-sized brown leather handbag with a gold zip. There's a small scratch on the front.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Good — that will help us identify it. What was inside the bag?

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: My purse with some cash and cards, my phone, and a pair of reading glasses.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: Was there any identification in the bag — like a passport or a driving licence?

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: Yes, my driving licence was in my purse.

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: I'll file a report now. If someone hands it in, we'll contact you. What is the best number to reach you on?

body.speaker: Passenger
body.text: My mobile number is 07712 345 678. How long does it usually take to find lost items?

body.speaker: Officer
body.text: It varies, but most items that are found are returned within forty-eight hours. Try not to worry.

annotations.meaningVi: Tôi nghĩ tôi đã bỏ quên
annotations.exampleEn: I think I left my umbrella on the bus.

annotations.meaningVi: nhận ra rằng nó đã bị mất
annotations.exampleEn: I only noticed my wallet was missing when I reached the checkout.
She bought a medium-sized suitcase for her trip.
The jacket has a gold zip on the front pocket.

annotations.meaningVi: nhận dạng / xác định đây là vật đó
annotations.exampleEn: The unique marking helped police identify the stolen bicycle.
My grandmother uses reading glasses for small print.
You need to show your driving licence if you are stopped by police.

annotations.meaningVi: lập biên bản / nộp báo cáo
annotations.exampleEn: After the accident, the driver filed a report with the police.

annotations.meaningVi: nộp lại / giao nộp (vật nhặt được)
annotations.exampleEn: A kind passenger handed in the lost wallet to the bus driver.
We'll contact you as soon as the package arrives.

annotations.meaningVi: Tuỳ từng trường hợp / Không nhất định
annotations.exampleEn: The time it takes to process an application varies.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for recent events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ đơn dùng để mô tả những sự kiện đã xảy ra và kết thúc trong quá khứ gần. Ví dụ: 'I left my handbag on the train.' / 'I got off at Victoria Station.' / 'I noticed it was missing.' Dạng này quan trọng khi khai báo với cảnh sát hoặc kể về sự cố vừa xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use Past Simple to describe events that happened and are now complete. Examples: 'I left my bag on the train.', 'I got off at Victoria Station.', 'I noticed it was missing.' This tense is essential when reporting a recent problem to an official.

grammarNotes.title: First conditional for future possibilities
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1 dùng 'If + hiện tại đơn, will + verb' để nói về những khả năng có thể xảy ra trong tương lai. Ví dụ: 'If someone hands it in, we'll contact you.' Đây là cách thông thường để nói về hành động phụ thuộc vào điều kiện xảy ra trong tương lai.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the First Conditional (If + present simple, will + verb) for real possibilities in the future. Example: 'If someone hands it in, we'll contact you.' This shows that the result (contacting) will only happen if the condition (finding the bag) is met.

translationVi:
Hành khách: Xin lỗi, thưa cảnh sát. Tôi nghĩ tôi đã bỏ quên túi xách trên tàu.
Cảnh sát: Tôi rất tiếc khi nghe vậy. Bạn phát hiện ra nó bị mất khi nào?
Hành khách: Khoảng mười phút trước, khi tôi đến lối ra nhà ga.
Cảnh sát: Bạn đi tàu nào — bạn có nhớ số hiệu hay tuyến không?
Hành khách: Tôi đi tuyến Circle Line. Tôi xuống ở Ga Victoria khoảng mười lăm phút trước.
Cảnh sát: Cảm ơn. Bạn có thể mô tả chiếc túi cho tôi không?
Hành khách: Đó là một chiếc túi xách da màu nâu cỡ vừa với dây kéo màu vàng. Có một vết xước nhỏ ở mặt trước.
Cảnh sát: Tốt — điều đó sẽ giúp chúng tôi nhận dạng nó. Bên trong túi có gì?
Hành khách: Ví của tôi có một ít tiền mặt và thẻ, điện thoại của tôi và một cặp kính đọc sách.
Cảnh sát: Trong túi có giấy tờ tuỳ thân nào không — như hộ chiếu hay giấy phép lái xe?
Hành khách: Có, giấy phép lái xe của tôi ở trong ví.
Cảnh sát: Tôi sẽ lập biên bản ngay bây giờ. Nếu ai đó nộp lại, chúng tôi sẽ liên hệ với bạn. Số điện thoại tốt nhất để liên lạc với bạn là gì?
Hành khách: Số điện thoại di động của tôi là 07712 345 678. Thông thường mất bao lâu để tìm thấy đồ bị mất?
Cảnh sát: Tuỳ từng trường hợp, nhưng hầu hết các vật tìm thấy đều được trả lại trong vòng bốn mươi tám giờ. Đừng lo lắng quá.

questions.prompt: Where does the passenger think she left her bag?
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'I think I left my handbag on the train.'
questions.hint: The passenger states the location at the very beginning.

questions.prompt: When did the passenger notice the bag was missing?
options: About ten minutes ago at the station exit
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'About ten minutes ago, when I arrived at the station exit.'
questions.hint: The passenger says where she was when she noticed.

questions.prompt: Which train line was the passenger on?
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'I was on the Circle Line.'
questions.hint: The line is named after a shape.

questions.prompt: Where did the passenger get off the train?
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'I got off at Victoria Station.'
questions.hint: The station is named after a British queen.

questions.explanation: The passenger describes it as 'a medium-sized brown leather handbag.'
questions.hint: The material and colour are both mentioned.

questions.prompt: What colour is the zip on the bag?
questions.explanation: The passenger says the bag has 'a gold zip.'

questions.prompt: What identifying feature does the bag have?
options: A small scratch on the front
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'There's a small scratch on the front.'
questions.hint: It is a minor mark on one side of the bag.

questions.prompt: Which item inside the bag helps identify the passenger?
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'my driving licence was in my purse.'
questions.hint: It is an official document that identifies its owner.

questions.prompt: What will the officer do if someone finds the bag?
options: Put it in the lost property office
Post it to the passenger's address
questions.explanation: The officer says 'If someone hands it in, we'll contact you.'
questions.hint: The officer gives a specific action in a conditional sentence.

questions.prompt: What number does the passenger give to the officer?
questions.explanation: The passenger says 'My mobile number is 07712 345 678.'
questions.hint: It is a portable phone number.

questions.prompt: How long does the officer say it usually takes to return found items?
questions.explanation: The officer says 'most items that are found are returned within forty-eight hours.'
questions.hint: A specific number of hours is mentioned.

Officer: I'm sorry to hear that. When did you notice it was {{b2}}?

Officer: Which train were you on — do you remember the number or the line?

Passenger: My purse with some cash and cards, my phone, and a pair of reading {{b6}}.
Officer: Was there any identification in the bag — like a passport or a driving {{b7}}?
Passenger: Yes, my driving licence was in my purse.
Officer: I'll {{b8}} a report now. If someone hands it in, we'll contact you. What is the best number to reach you on?

Officer: It varies, but most items that are found are returned within forty-eight {{b10}}. Try not to worry.

blanks.explanation: 'left' — to leave something means to accidentally forget it in a place.
'missing' — when something is not where it should be, it is missing.
'describe' — to describe means to say what something looks like.
'zip' — a fastening device on bags and clothing that opens and closes.
'identify' — to identify means to recognise and confirm what something is.
'glasses' — reading glasses are used to help see small text more clearly.
'licence' — a driving licence is an official document allowing someone to drive.
'file' — to file a report means to officially record the details of an incident.
'items' — lost items refers to the things that have been misplaced or forgotten.
'hours' — the officer says most items are returned within forty-eight hours.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The passenger is worried about losing not just the bag but also identification documents and bank cards. In what ways does losing a wallet or handbag cause more problems than just losing physical items? What steps can people take to reduce the damage when important personal items are lost or stolen?
